subject: [PATCH 2/2] block: invalidate cached plug timestamp after task switch

2026-06-16.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug where the cached plug timestamp is not invalidated after a task switch. The existing invalidation mechanism only covers explicit plug flushes and schedule/preemption paths, but not in-kernel preemption paths like preempt_schedule(). As a result, a task can reuse a stale plug->cur_ktime value after it is scheduled back in, leading to incorrect throttle decisions and QoS samples. To fix this, the patch moves the invalidation to finish_task_switch(), which runs for the scheduled-in task after every actual context switch.
